title: The Mystery of the Missing Marbles
synopsis: When Rohan's prized marble collection disappears from his classroom, he teams up with his friends Maya and Carlos to solve the mystery. Their investigation leads them through clues, red herrings, and unexpected discoveries, teaching them about friendship, assumptions, and the importance of understanding others.

chapters.title: The Marble Collection
chapters.content:
Rohan carefully arranged his marbles on his desk, making sure each one caught the morning sunlight streaming through the classroom window. His collection was his pride and joy – twenty-three beautiful glass spheres in every color imaginable. There was his favorite blue one with swirls like ocean waves, a green one that looked like it held tiny emeralds inside, and a special golden marble his grandmother had given him last summer.

"Wow, Rohan! Those are so cool!" said Maya, sliding into the seat next to him. Her dark curls bounced as she leaned closer to examine the marbles. "Can I hold the sparkly purple one?"

"Sure, but be super careful," Rohan said, handing it to her gently. "That one's really special. I found it at the antique store downtown."

Carlos plopped down in front of them, spinning around in his chair. "Dude, you bring those marbles to school like every day. Aren't you worried about losing them?"

"Nah," Rohan shook his head. "I keep them safe in my desk during class. Nobody would take them." He gathered up the marbles and placed them carefully in their soft velvet pouch, then locked them in his desk drawer.

Mrs. Peterson clapped her hands. "Good morning, class! Time to put away your things and get ready for math."

Rohan patted his desk drawer one more time, feeling good about his treasures being safe inside. He had no idea that by lunchtime, everything would change.

illustrations.description: A sunny elementary classroom with golden morning light streaming through tall windows. Rohan, a boy with warm brown skin and black hair, sits at his desk arranging colorful glass marbles that sparkle in the sunlight. Maya, a girl with dark curly hair and light brown skin, leans over to admire them while Carlos, a boy with tan skin and short dark hair, spins in his chair nearby. The scene has warm, inviting colors with detailed classroom elements like books, pencils, and plants on windowsills.
illustrations.alt: Rohan showing his marble collection to Maya and Carlos in their sunny classroom

chapters.title: Gone!
chapters.content:
After lunch, Rohan rushed back to his seat, excited to show his marbles to Emma, who had missed seeing them that morning. He pulled out his key and unlocked his desk drawer, reaching inside for the familiar velvet pouch.

His hand found nothing but empty space.

"What?" Rohan's heart started beating faster. He pulled the drawer all the way out, searching frantically through pencils, erasers, and crumpled papers. "No, no, no!"

"What's wrong?" Maya asked, seeing his panicked expression.

"My marbles! They're gone!" Rohan's voice cracked. "Someone took my marble collection!"

Carlos jumped up from his seat. "Are you sure? Maybe you put them somewhere else?"

"I'm positive!" Rohan said, his eyes filling with tears. "I put them right here before math class. The drawer was locked, and now they're just... gone!"

Several other kids gathered around, including Emma, who had flowing red hair and freckles across her nose. "Maybe they fell out?" she suggested, getting down on her hands and knees to look under the desk.

"Someone stole them," Rohan said miserably. "My grandma's golden marble was in there. What am I going to tell her?"

Maya put her hand on his shoulder. "Don't worry, Rohan. We're going to find them. Right, Carlos?"

"Absolutely," Carlos nodded firmly. "We'll be like real detectives. Nobody messes with our friend's marbles!"

"But who would take them?" Rohan asked, looking around the classroom with new suspicion. "And how did they get into my locked drawer?"

"That's what we're going to find out," Maya said with determination. "The mystery of the missing marbles starts now!"

illustrations.description: Rohan sits at his desk with his drawer pulled completely out, his face showing shock and distress as he realizes his marbles are missing. Maya stands beside him with a comforting hand on his shoulder while Carlos leans over to look in the empty drawer. Emma kneels on the floor searching under the desk. The classroom lighting is softer now, creating shadows that add to the mysterious mood. Scattered school supplies are visible in the open drawer.
illustrations.alt: Rohan discovering his marble collection is missing while his friends try to help

chapters.title: The Investigation Begins
chapters.content:
During afternoon recess, Rohan, Maya, and Carlos huddled together under the big oak tree in the schoolyard. Maya had brought a small notebook and was writing down everything they knew.

"Okay," Maya said, tapping her pencil against her lips. "Let's think about this like real detectives. Who had the chance to take the marbles?"

"Well, everyone in our class was there during math," Carlos said. "But Rohan's drawer was locked, right?"

"Yeah, but maybe someone saw where I keep my key," Rohan said sadly. "I always put it in my pencil case."

"So anyone who watched you could know where to find it," Maya wrote this down. "What about motive? Why would someone want your marbles?"

"Because they're awesome?" Carlos suggested. "I mean, they're really beautiful. Maybe someone was jealous."

Rohan thought about this. "Tyler was looking at them yesterday and said he wished he had some like them. And Sarah asked if she could trade her Pokemon cards for a few, but I said no."

"Aha!" Maya said, writing furiously. "Tyler and Sarah are our first suspects. Anyone else?"

"What about the new kid, Alex?" Carlos asked. "He sits near Rohan and seems kind of... I don't know, sneaky?"

"That's not fair," Maya frowned. "We don't even know Alex yet. Just because he's new doesn't make him sneaky."

"You're right," Carlos said, looking embarrassed. "Sorry. I just want to help Rohan get his marbles back."

"We all do," Maya said. "But we have to be fair and look for real evidence. Let's start by talking to Tyler and Sarah after recess."

Rohan nodded, feeling a little better. At least his friends were helping him. Together, they would solve this mystery.

illustrations.description: Three children sit in a circle under a large oak tree during recess. Maya, with her notebook open, leads their detective meeting while autumn leaves cast dappled shadows around them. The school playground is visible in the background with other children playing. The golden afternoon light filters through the tree branches, creating a cozy investigative atmosphere. Maya looks focused and determined, Carlos appears thoughtful, and Rohan seems worried but hopeful.
illustrations.alt: Maya, Rohan, and Carlos planning their investigation under the oak tree

chapters.title: Following the Clues
chapters.content:
After recess, the three friends approached Tyler at his locker. Tyler was a tall boy with blonde hair who loved to collect things – rocks, bottle caps, and baseball cards.

"Hey Tyler," Maya said casually. "Did you see anything weird in our classroom today?"

Tyler shrugged. "Not really. Why?"

"Someone took Rohan's marble collection," Carlos said bluntly.

Tyler's eyes widened. "Seriously? That's terrible! I loved looking at those marbles. Who would do something like that?"

Rohan studied Tyler's face carefully. He seemed genuinely surprised and upset about the news.

"You don't know anything about it?" Maya asked.

"No way!" Tyler said firmly. "I would never steal someone's collection. I know how it feels to lose something important." He paused. "But I did see something kind of strange."

"What?" all three friends asked at once.

"During math class, I saw someone walking around near the desks when Mrs. Peterson was writing on the board. But I couldn't tell who it was from where I was sitting."

"What did they look like?" Rohan asked eagerly.

"I just saw them from behind. They had dark hair and were wearing a red shirt, I think."

The friends exchanged glances. Several kids in their class had dark hair and wore red shirts sometimes.

"Thanks, Tyler," Maya said. "That's actually really helpful."

As they walked away, Carlos whispered, "I don't think it was him. He seemed really honest."

"Agreed," Maya said. "Let's go find Sarah."

But when they found Sarah by the art room, she was crying. "I can't find my lunch money anywhere!" she sobbed. "Mom gave me five dollars this morning, and now it's gone too!"

Rohan, Maya, and Carlos looked at each other with growing concern. This wasn't just about marbles anymore. Someone was taking things from multiple students.

illustrations.description: Tyler stands at his school locker talking to the three friends in a school hallway lined with colorful lockers and student artwork on the walls. Tyler appears genuine and concerned as he shares his information. The hallway has warm lighting and shows other students in the background. His body language is open and helpful, not defensive or suspicious.
illustrations.alt: Tyler talking to the detective trio at the school lockers

chapters.title: More Missing Things
chapters.content:
Word spread quickly that things were going missing from their classroom. By the next morning, three more kids had reported missing items: Kevin couldn't find his special pen, Emma's bookmark had vanished, and Lily's small stuffed animal was gone from her backpack.

"This is getting serious," Maya said, adding the new information to her detective notebook. "We're not just looking for one thief anymore. We're looking for someone who's been taking stuff for days!"

"But why would someone take a bookmark and a stuffed animal?" Carlos wondered. "Those aren't worth much money."

"Maybe it's not about money," Rohan said thoughtfully. "Maybe someone just likes taking things that belong to other people."

During morning work time, Mrs. Peterson made an announcement. "Class, I've heard that some of your belongings have gone missing. If anyone knows anything about this, please come talk to me privately. We need to solve this problem together."

Rohan noticed Alex, the new student, shift uncomfortably in his seat. Alex had joined their class just two weeks ago and was still pretty quiet. He had short black hair and usually wore simple clothes. Rohan realized he didn't know much about Alex at all.

"Did you see that?" Carlos whispered, nodding toward Alex.

"See what?" Maya asked.

"Alex looked weird when Mrs. Peterson was talking about the missing stuff."

Maya frowned. "Lots of people look uncomfortable when teachers talk about stealing. That doesn't mean anything."

But Rohan was watching Alex more carefully now. During lunch, he saw Alex sitting alone, as usual. Alex kept looking around nervously and barely touched his sandwich.

"Maybe we should just talk to him," Rohan suggested. "If he didn't take anything, then we can cross him off our suspect list. And if he's just scared or knows something, maybe we can help."

"Good idea," Maya agreed. "Being a good detective means talking to everyone, not just accusing people."

illustrations.description: The classroom scene shows Mrs. Peterson addressing the class while students sit at their desks. Alex, a boy with short black hair and olive skin, sits alone looking uncomfortable and nervous. Other students appear concerned and whisper to each other. The classroom has educational posters on the walls and morning sunlight streaming through windows, but there's a subtle tension in the air. Maya is shown taking notes while Rohan and Carlos observe Alex's reaction.
illustrations.alt: Mrs. Peterson addressing the class about missing items while Alex looks uncomfortable

chapters.title: An Unexpected Discovery
chapters.content:
During lunch, Rohan, Maya, and Carlos approached Alex's table. He was sitting by himself, picking at his peanut butter sandwich and looking sad.

"Hey Alex," Maya said gently. "Mind if we sit with you?"

Alex looked up, surprised. "Um, okay I guess."

They sat down, and for a moment, nobody knew what to say. Finally, Rohan spoke up. "So, you're from Portland, right? Do you like it here?"

"It's okay," Alex said quietly. "Different from my old school."

"Moving is hard," Maya said kindly. "I moved here two years ago, and I was really nervous about making friends."

Alex nodded. "Everyone here already has their friend groups. It's hard to fit in."

Carlos felt bad about calling Alex sneaky earlier. "You could hang out with us sometime. We like exploring and solving puzzles and stuff."

"Really?" Alex's face lit up a little. Then it fell again. "I don't know if I'm going to be here much longer anyway."

"Why not?" Rohan asked.

"My dad might lose his job," Alex said in a small voice. "If he does, we'll have to move again. That's why I don't really try to make friends. It just makes leaving harder."

The three friends felt terrible. They had been so focused on their investigation that they hadn't noticed Alex was struggling.

"That must be really scary," Maya said softly.

"Yeah," Alex nodded. "And now with all this stuff about things being stolen, everyone's looking at me funny. Being the new kid is hard enough without people thinking you're a thief."

"We don't think you're a thief," Rohan said quickly. "We're just trying to figure out what happened to everyone's stuff."

Alex looked at them seriously. "I didn't take anything, I promise. But... I think I might know who did."

illustrations.description: Four children sit at a round lunch table in the school cafeteria. Alex looks sad and vulnerable as he shares his story, while Maya, Rohan, and Carlos listen with growing empathy and understanding. Lunch trays with half-eaten food sit on the table. The cafeteria background shows other students eating and talking. The lighting is soft and warm, emphasizing the emotional connection forming between the children.
illustrations.alt: Alex opening up to his new friends during lunch

chapters.title: The Real Culprit
chapters.content:
"You know who's been taking things?" Maya asked, leaning forward with interest.

Alex nodded slowly. "I think so. But you're not going to believe me."

"Try us," Carlos said.

Alex took a deep breath. "It's not a person at all. It's Mrs. Peterson's classroom hamster, Peanut."

The three friends stared at him. "The hamster?" Rohan said incredulously.

"I know it sounds crazy," Alex said quickly. "But hear me out. Yesterday, I stayed after class to ask Mrs. Peterson a question, and I saw Peanut out of his cage. He was carrying something shiny in his mouth – it looked like one of your marbles, Rohan."

"But how would Peanut get out of his cage?" Maya asked, though she was already writing in her notebook.

"The latch has been loose for weeks," Alex explained. "I noticed it because I watch Peanut a lot during class. Sometimes when he climbs around, he bumps the door and it opens a little."

Carlos's eyes widened. "So Peanut gets out at night or during recess and takes shiny things back to his cage?"

"Exactly! Hamsters love to collect things for their nests. And all the missing items are small and either shiny or soft – perfect for a hamster to carry."

Rohan felt a surge of hope. "So my marbles might still be in the classroom somewhere?"

"Probably hidden around Peanut's cage area," Alex said. "I've been watching him for days, trying to figure out if I was right before I said anything."

Maya stood up. "There's only one way to find out. Let's go check the classroom right now."

"But what if we're wrong?" Carlos asked. "What if it really is a person stealing things?"

"Then we'll keep investigating," Maya said firmly. "But Alex's theory makes sense. And it explains why the thief could get into locked desks – Peanut is small enough to squeeze through tiny spaces."

The four friends hurried toward their classroom, hoping to solve the mystery once and for all.

illustrations.description: Alex gestures excitedly as he explains his hamster theory to the other three children, who look surprised but intrigued. In the background, through a doorway, we can see their classroom with Peanut's cage visible on a shelf. The scene captures the moment of revelation, with golden afternoon light and expressions of growing understanding on the children's faces. Alex appears more confident and included now that he's sharing his important discovery.
illustrations.alt: Alex explaining his hamster theory to his surprised friends

chapters.title: Peanut's Secret Stash
chapters.content:
The classroom was empty when they arrived. Mrs. Peterson was in a meeting, and the janitor, Mr. Williams, let them in when they explained they needed to get something from their desks.

"Just be quick, kids," Mr. Williams said with a smile. "And don't disturb Peanut's naptime."

They approached Peanut's cage carefully. The little golden hamster was indeed sleeping, curled up in a corner that looked much lumpier than usual.

"Look at all that bedding," Maya whispered. "It's way more than normal."

"And look," Alex pointed to the cage latch. "See how it's barely hanging on?"

Rohan peered closer and gasped. "I can see something blue and shiny in his bedding! That's my ocean marble!"

Very gently, Maya opened the cage and carefully moved some of the bedding aside. Hidden underneath Peanut's collection were Rohan's marbles, Sarah's lunch money, Kevin's special pen, Emma's bookmark, and Lily's small stuffed animal.

"Oh my gosh," Carlos breathed. "Alex was right! Peanut's been collecting everything!"

Peanut woke up and chittered at them, as if protesting the disturbance of his treasure hoard.

"Don't worry, Peanut," Rohan said softly, carefully retrieving his marbles. "We're not mad at you. You were just following your hamster instincts."

"We need to tell Mrs. Peterson and everyone else," Maya said, helping gather the other missing items. "They'll be so relieved to know it wasn't a person stealing things."

"And we need to fix Peanut's cage so this doesn't happen again," Alex added. "Maybe we can ask Mr. Williams to help make it more secure."

As they collected the last of the missing items, Rohan looked at Alex gratefully. "Thank you for figuring this out. You're really smart, and you saved the day."

Alex blushed happily. "I'm just glad I could help. And... thanks for believing me about the hamster. I know it sounded pretty weird."

"The best detectives always consider every possibility," Maya said with a grin. "Even the weird ones."

illustrations.description: Four children crowd around Peanut's hamster cage, carefully examining the secret collection hidden in the bedding. Peanut, a small golden hamster, sits among his treasures including colorful marbles, coins, and small objects. The classroom setting shows afternoon sunlight streaming through windows. The children's faces show amazement and relief at solving the mystery. Maya gently moves bedding while Rohan points excitedly at his recovered marbles.
illustrations.alt: The children discovering Peanut's secret treasure collection in his cage

chapters.title: Mystery Solved
chapters.content:
When Mrs. Peterson returned from her meeting, she found four excited children waiting for her with all the missing items neatly arranged on her desk.

"My goodness!" she exclaimed. "You found everything! How did you solve the mystery?"

The friends took turns explaining their investigation, with Alex shyly sharing how he had figured out Peanut was the culprit. Mrs. Peterson listened with growing amazement and pride.

"Alex, that was incredibly observant of you," she said. "And all of you showed excellent teamwork and problem-solving skills."

Soon, word spread through the school that the mystery was solved. Tyler, Sarah, Emma, Kevin, and Lily were thrilled to get their belongings back.

"I can't believe it was Peanut all along!" Sarah laughed, looking at her recovered lunch money.

"He's like a tiny furry burglar," Kevin said, examining his special pen to make sure it wasn't damaged.

Emma giggled. "At least he took good care of everything. My bookmark doesn't even have teeth marks on it."

Mr. Williams came by to fix Peanut's cage with a proper secure latch. "This little guy is quite the escape artist," he chuckled. "But now he'll have to stick to his regular hamster food instead of stealing your treasures."

As the excitement died down, Rohan carefully arranged his marbles on his desk one more time. But this time, he had an idea.

"Hey Alex," he called out. "Want to see my marble collection up close? I can teach you about the different types of glass and where I found each one."

Alex's face broke into the biggest smile anyone had seen from him since he'd arrived at the school. "Really? That would be awesome!"

Maya and Carlos joined them, and soon Alex was handling the marbles as carefully as Rohan did, listening intently to the story behind each one.

"You know," Carlos said, "we make a pretty good detective team. Maybe we should look for more mysteries to solve."

"As long as they don't involve my marbles going missing again," Rohan laughed.

"Deal," Maya said, and all four friends shook hands on it.

From that day on, Alex never sat alone at lunch again. And Peanut, safely secured in his properly latched cage, had to content himself with his regular hamster toys – though he still eyed the shiny objects in the classroom with obvious longing.

The mystery of the missing marbles was solved, but more importantly, a new friendship had been found.

illustrations.description: The classroom filled with happy children and Mrs. Peterson, all gathered around as the missing items are returned to their owners. Alex stands proudly with his new friends while Peanut sits in his newly secured cage in the background. The scene is warm and celebratory, with golden afternoon light streaming through windows. Everyone looks relieved and joyful, and there's a sense of community and friendship filling the space.
illustrations.alt: Everyone celebrating the solved mystery and returned belongings

metadata.illustrationStyle: Warm, inviting adventure illustration style. Earthy tones, golden light. Detailed environments with hidden details kids can discover.

questions.question: What was special about Rohan's marble collection?
choices: It included a golden marble from his grandmother
All the marbles were the same size
He found them all at school
questions.explanation: Rohan had a special golden marble that his grandmother gave him last summer, which was one of his most treasured marbles.

questions.question: How did the thief get into Rohan's locked desk drawer?
choices: The thief was actually too small to need the key
They picked the lock with a paperclip
questions.explanation: The 'thief' was Peanut the hamster, who was small enough to squeeze through tiny spaces and didn't need to unlock the drawer.

questions.question: Who figured out that Peanut was the real culprit?
questions.explanation: Alex was the one who observed Peanut escaping from his cage and carrying shiny objects, leading him to solve the mystery.

questions.question: Why was Alex hesitant to make friends at his new school?
choices: He was too shy to talk to anyone
He thought the other kids didn't like him
His family might have to move again soon
questions.explanation: Alex was hesitant to make friends because his dad might lose his job, which would mean the family would have to move again, making it hard to leave friends behind.

questions.question: What kind of items did Peanut collect in his cage?
questions.explanation: Peanut collected small items that were either shiny (like marbles and coins) or soft (like stuffed animals), which is typical hamster hoarding behavior.

questions.question: How did Peanut escape from his cage?
choices: The latch was loose and would open when he climbed around
questions.explanation: The cage latch had been loose for weeks, and when Peanut climbed around, he would accidentally bump the door open.

questions.question: What did the friends learn from this mystery?
choices: Don't make assumptions about people and include everyone
questions.explanation: The friends learned not to make assumptions about people (like thinking Alex was suspicious just for being new) and the importance of including others and being understanding.

pages.title: Detective Word Search
pages.description: Help solve the mystery by finding these words hidden in the puzzle!

pages.title: Missing Items Match
pages.description: Match each missing item with its owner!

pages.title: Peanut's Escape Route
pages.description: Help Peanut find his way through the classroom to collect treasures, then back to his cage!
